title: China-to-Côte d'Ivoire BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Grid Basis and Site-Specific Settings
rows.target_requirement: Côte d'Ivoire participates in the West African Power Pool and the national grid is planned and operated as part of a regional electricity-market context. China and Côte d'Ivoire both use a 50 Hz design basis for normal BESS PCS and transformer screening, but interconnection acceptance still depends on the specific Côte d'Ivoire point of connection and utility/project settings.
target_standards: Côte d'Ivoire grid and WAPP regional electricity-market context
Project-specific frequency and voltage operating windows
Utility interconnection and commissioning settings
rows.china_equivalent: China factory PCS settings and 50 Hz design data are useful starting evidence. They still need site validation for Côte d'Ivoire voltage class, transformer taps, relay settings, harmonics, grounding, SCADA, grid-code operating windows, and commissioning tests.
rows.gap: Do not rely only on the 50 Hz match. Provide Côte d'Ivoire-specific protection settings, power-quality study, short-circuit study, transformer configuration, SCADA mapping, commissioning plan, and site acceptance records.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected BESS where utility interconnection, grid-code, EPC, owner, insurer, or commissioning approval applies; detailed settings are project-specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or contract-specific for China grid-connected projects through grid-company technical review, connection agreement, and commissioning acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] The 50 Hz match reduces equipment-screening friction but is not a Côte d'Ivoire interconnection approval; site settings and commissioning remain separate requirements.

rows.requirement: China GB 44240-2024 / GB/T 36276-2023 Baseline vs Côte d'Ivoire Evidence
rows.target_requirement: For Côte d'Ivoire, China GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 are useful manufacturer baseline evidence but are not, by themselves, Côte d'Ivoire legal approvals. A Côte d'Ivoire dossier should translate those reports into IEC, CODINORM, ANARE-CI / utility, fire-safety, and transport evidence as required by the shipment and project route.
target_standards: CODINORM acceptance route where applicable
IEC 62619 and IEC 62933 evidence where requested
ANARE-CI / CIE / CI-ENERGIES project review
ONPC fire-safety review
UN 38.3 transport evidence
rows.china_equivalent: GB 44240-2024 is the key China mandatory safety baseline for electrochemical energy-storage stations where applicable, while GB/T 36276-2023 is a widely used recommended lithium-ion battery standard for electrical energy storage. They should be clause-mapped to IEC 62619, IEC 62933, and project-specific Côte d'Ivoire requirements.
rows.gap: Create a master evidence matrix showing which China GB documents are formal certificates, which are test reports, which are voluntary standards, and which Côte d'Ivoire reviewer must accept each item. Add French translations for summaries, nameplate data, installation limits, safety warnings, and emergency procedures.

mandatory_status.target: Not mandatory as China GB documents in Côte d'Ivoire unless incorporated by CODINORM, permit, tender, contract, insurer, lender, utility, or project owner; accepted value is evidence-based and route-specific
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where applicable in China; GB/T 36276-2023 and GB/T 36558-2023 are recommended unless incorporated by regulation or contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China GB evidence is a strong technical baseline, not a Côte d'Ivoire approval. Convert it into a reviewer-specific IEC, CODINORM, ANARE-CI / utility, fire, and transport dossier.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation / Standardization Administration of China

rows.requirement: CODINORM and IEC 62619 / IEC 62933 Evidence for BESS
rows.target_requirement: CODINORM is Côte d'Ivoire's national standards and product-certification body. For BESS, the official public material reviewed did not identify a single CODINORM mandatory product certificate specific to stationary lithium BESS. IEC 62619:2022 battery-safety evidence and IEC 62933-series system evidence should therefore be treated as likely technical evidence for a CODINORM, tender, insurer, lender, customs, or project-owner review unless CODINORM, a contract, or a permit expressly makes a specific edition mandatory.
target_standards: CODINORM normalisation and product certification services
IEC 62933 series
rows.china_equivalent: China-side BESS evidence commonly includes GB 44240-2024 for electrochemical energy-storage station safety and GB/T 36276-2023 for lithium-ion batteries for electrical energy storage. These may support a technical crosswalk, but they should not be assumed to replace IEC-numbered reports or CODINORM acceptance.
rows.gap: Confirm the HS codes and CODINORM import/conformity route before shipment. Prepare an English or French clause matrix from GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 to IEC 62619:2022 and the relevant IEC 62933 parts, clearly separating formal IEC test reports from China-only GB evidence.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific or mandatory only where CODINORM, customs, permit, tender, insurer, lender, utility, or contract calls up the standard or conformity route
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where applicable in China; GB/T 36276-2023 and GB/T 36558-2023 are recommended unless incorporated by regulation or contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat IEC 62619 / IEC 62933 as required technical evidence only when CODINORM, customs, the buyer, or a project approval route calls it up; do not assume China GB reports are automatically accepted.

rows.requirement: ONPC Fire-Safety Review for BESS Installations
rows.target_requirement: The Office National de la Protection Civile (ONPC) is Côte d'Ivoire's civil-protection authority and public official material references fire-safety training, approved fire-safety and first-aid firms, and safety-certificate inspection activity for establishments open to the public. BESS projects should expect local fire-safety review at site level, especially for containerized lithium batteries, indoor electrical rooms, public or industrial premises, and emergency-response planning.
target_standards: ONPC civil-protection and fire-safety oversight
Local fire-safety certificate / inspection requirements where applicable
Project fire-protection design and emergency-response plan
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly uses GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, GB/T 36558-2023, and project fire-safety documentation for electrochemical energy storage. These documents may support technical due diligence but do not replace ONPC, local authority, insurer, or site-owner fire-safety acceptance.
rows.gap: Prepare a French fire-safety dossier with site layout, separation distances, container ventilation, thermal-runaway mitigation, gas detection where relevant, fire detection and suppression design, emergency shutdown logic, firefighting access, SDS, and emergency response procedures.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where local fire-safety certificate, building/site permit, public/industrial premises inspection, insurer requirement, or project-owner approval applies; exact route is site-specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on China project approval, GB 44240-2024 applicability, and local fire review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Côte d'Ivoire BESS fire safety should be treated as a site-approval issue. China GB safety files are useful support but do not replace ONPC, local authority, insurer, or owner review.

source.publisher: Office National de la Protection Civile (ONPC)

rows.requirement: ANARE-CI, CIE, and CI-ENERGIES Grid / Storage Project Review
rows.target_requirement: ANARE-CI states that it controls compliance with electricity laws, regulations, authorizations, and conventions, while CIE operates the national public electricity service and CI-ENERGIES manages state electricity-sector assets and network investment planning. A grid-connected BESS should therefore be treated as a project-level electricity-sector approval and interconnection matter, not just an imported battery product.
target_standards: Côte d'Ivoire Electricity Code Law No. 2014-132
ANARE-CI electricity-sector regulation and authorization oversight
CIE / CI-ENERGIES interconnection, operation, and commissioning requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China-side grid and system evidence commonly includes GB/T 36558-2023 and project grid-company acceptance records. These documents can support engineering review but do not substitute for Côte d'Ivoire interconnection studies, protection settings, commissioning tests, or ANARE-CI / utility acceptance.
rows.gap: Prepare a Côte d'Ivoire-specific interconnection dossier covering single-line diagrams, grid model, relay settings, anti-islanding, fault ride-through where requested, harmonics, grounding, metering, SCADA/telecontrol, commissioning tests, and operating procedures in French.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected projects where electricity-sector authorization, concession, interconnection agreement, utility review, or commissioning acceptance applies; details are project-specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or contract-specific for China grid-connected projects through grid-company technical review, connection agreement, and commissioning acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A BESS battery certificate does not close the Côte d'Ivoire grid gap; ANARE-CI, CIE, CI-ENERGIES, and project-specific commissioning expectations should be addressed before energization.

rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells and batteries in BESS shipments must be supported by UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, Part III, subsection 38.3 evidence for international transport. Côte d'Ivoire does not create a public BESS-specific exception in the official material reviewed; sea shipments normally require IMDG dangerous-goods documentation, SDS, proper UN number classification, packaging evidence, and carrier acceptance.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, Part III, subsection 38.3
IMDG Code for sea transport of dangerous goods
Carrier and port dangerous-goods documentation requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese BESS exporters commonly hold UN 38.3 reports for cells and battery packs, but GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 are product or project safety standards and do not replace transport-classification evidence.
china_standards: UN 38.3 test report for exported lithium cells and batteries
rows.gap: Verify UN 38.3 reports for each shipped cell and battery configuration, confirm SDS and UN number declarations, and align the dangerous-goods declaration with the actual containerized BESS shipping state before booking vessel space.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for international transport of lithium cells and batteries; Côte d'Ivoire import does not remove carrier, IMDG, or port dangerous-goods obligations
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for international export transport of lithium cells and batteries from China

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] UN 38.3 transport evidence is separate from IEC or GB battery safety evidence and should be verified for every BESS cell and battery configuration shipped to Côte d'Ivoire.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
